calendar.matters: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Requests For Production, Set Two And Request For Sanctions

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Requests For Production, Set Two And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-03-2026 Continued To Jun-26-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Requests For Production, Set Two And Request For Sanctions.

Continued on court's own motion to June 26, 2026.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kiersten Wiens, Esq. 310-622-4278 Blackstone Law for Plaintiff.
Ryan Finn, Esq. 415-369-3555 Ogletree Deakins for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Mr. Finn agrees with TR; RFP 40 and 41 are unsupplemented, the TR had a few typos.
Court poses questions; no oppositon filed.
Ms. Wiens argues; provided responses, reconsider sanctions, states timeline of discovery.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Delta Dental of California's unopposed Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records Issued To Plaintiff?S Former Employers; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JONATHAN DSOUZA'S Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records Issued To Plaintiff'S Former Employers; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiff Jonathan Dsouza's motion to quash deposition subpoenas for production of business records issued to his former employers is DENIED without prejudice.

California Rule of Court 3.1345 requires the inclusion of a separate statement for any motion involving the content of a discovery request, explicitly including a motion "to compel or to quash the production of documents or tangible things at a deposition." (CRC 3.1345(a)(5).) The separate statement must "provide[] all the information necessary to understand each discovery request and all the responses to it that are at issue" and must be "full and complete so that no person is required to review any other document in order to determine the full request and the full response." (CRC 3.1345(c).) Here, Plaintiff has not included a separate statement with his motion. The court denies Plaintiff's motion on the basis of Plaintiff's failure to comply with the separate statement requirement.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/SKF)
